summary: Reviewed the source diff adding an optional GitHub PR publication decision/evidence to pr-merge-readiness (docs, schema, evaluator, template, example fixture, tests, workflow). Schema/template/example/test fixtures are mutually consistent (github_publication added as a required top-level field across all four), and the evaluator gates correctly on the key risk surfaces: a 'publish' answer requires status=published plus a fully-shaped result.json, body/result files on disk, and a URL, while 'skip'/'defer' are non-blocking by design; the state classifier (_classify_state) keeps github-evidence blockers as blocked_missing_evidence and routes github-decision-only blockers to awaiting_human_decision without masking real check/review/finding failures. I found no P0/P1 false-acceptance path: the merge.acceptance hash binding and the fact that publication mutates the report (github_publication.status) make every problematic case fail closed (awaiting/blocked/rejected), as the added tests demonstrate. Candidate findings are clarity/consistency-level: (F1) the workflow requests merge.acceptance in both the preliminary human_merge_decision phase (before the binding report exists) and the final_human_merge_decision phase, without explicitly labeling the preliminary record as non-authoritative, and duplicate merge.acceptance handling is untested; (F2) final_readiness_gate validates before the final hash-bound decision is recorded with no re-validation phase shown after it; (F3) the documented redaction rules for the outward-facing github-publication.md comment body are unenforced; (F4) required_for_merge_readiness is informational and never consulted for gating; (F5) an absent github.publication decision is labeled 'invalid' rather than 'missing'. Packet-level mandatory evidence (diff, verification-gate report, freshness) is present and this is explicitly a focused source review, not a full acceptance gate. Three items could not be verified read-only and are listed as verification requests (unshown human-decisions schema, example hash/run_id recomputation, merge.acceptance matcher body).

findings.title: Preliminary vs final merge.acceptance: both phases require merge.acceptance, but the binding report does not exist at the preliminary phase, and duplicate-decision handling is untested
evidence: workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml: phase human_merge_decision sets human_interaction.required_decisions [merge.acceptance, github.publication], and the new phase final_human_merge_decision (runs_after final_readiness_gate) also sets required_decisions [merge.acceptance].

Phase ordering is human_merge_decision -> optional_github_publication -> readiness_report -> final_readiness_gate -> final_human_merge_decision; the readiness report whose report_hash the human merge acceptance must bind does not yet exist when the preliminary human_merge_decision phase requests merge.acceptance.

docs/pr-merge-readiness.md says the final step must 'record or refresh the final human merge decision with the exact report_hash', implying the preliminary merge.acceptance is non-binding, but neither workflow.yaml nor docs explicitly mark the preliminary merge.acceptance as non-authoritative intent vs the final hash-bound record (FPM-HUMAN-001).

No demonstrated false-accept: the evaluator binds merge.acceptance to report_hash + material_change_id and publication mutates the report (github_publication.status flips toward published), so a preliminary hash cannot match the final report and the gate fails closed; the risk is workflow clarity / implementation error, not over-acceptance.

Coverage gap: the added tests in tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py only vary a single merge.acceptance entry; the dual-phase design can produce two merge.acceptance records, and unlike _matching_github_publication_answer (which explicitly rejects len(matching) != 1) the merge.acceptance matcher body is not in the packet, so its duplicate handling is unverified.

findings.title: final_readiness_gate validates before the final hash-bound human merge decision is recorded, with no re-validation phase shown afterward
evidence: workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml: final_readiness_gate (kind: gate, scripts: validate_repo) runs_after readiness_report and precedes final_human_merge_decision; the hash-bound merge.acceptance is only recorded in final_human_merge_decision (runs_after final_readiness_gate).

At final_readiness_gate time the exact-hash acceptance does not yet exist, so the evaluator would classify awaiting_human_decision; no phase after final_human_merge_decision re-runs validation to reach accepted_merge_ready, so the workflow does not show where the final accepted state is computed (FPM-PHASE-001).

exit_criteria state 'Accepted merge-ready status has recorded human merge approval bound to the current material change and exact readiness report hash', but the workflow's final validating gate precedes that record.

Direction is fail-closed (under-accept, not false-accept); this does not prove the workflow cannot accept since the evaluator computes state on demand against the final artifacts, but the ordering/closure point is left implicit.

findings.title: Outward-facing github-publication.md redaction rules are documented but not enforced by the evaluator
evidence: docs/pr-merge-readiness.md requires that github-publication.md 'must not include raw Claude output, full reviewer reports, long command logs, private reasoning details or unnecessary absolute local paths.'

scripts/repo_validation/pr_merge_readiness.py _validate_github_publication_result only verifies body_path/result_path existence and result.json field equality (provider/tool/action/status/body_path) plus URL presence; it never inspects the content of github-publication.md.

The published artifact is outward-facing (public GitHub PR via gh pr comment), whereas local raw-output evidence is subject to a blocked_sensitive_raw_evidence gate; the outward comment body has no equivalent automated redaction check (publication_evidence_integrity / FPM-PUB-001).

Compensating control: the comment body is human-authored and the publish decision is human-confirmed, and reliable content scanning for 'raw output' is fuzzy, so this may be acceptable by design; flagged for relevance validation rather than as a hard blocker.

findings.title: github_publication.required_for_merge_readiness is informational only and never consulted for gating
evidence: schemas/pr-merge-readiness-report.schema.json declares required_for_merge_readiness (boolean) as a required github_publication field.

scripts/repo_validation/pr_merge_readiness.py copies required_for_merge_readiness into github_publication_summary but uses it in no blocking conditional; gating is driven solely by the human answer (publish -> evidence required; skip/defer -> non-blocking).

tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py test_requested_github_publication_with_evidence_can_pass accepts with required_for_merge_readiness False, confirming the flag does not relax or strengthen enforcement; a reader could wrongly assume setting it true enforces publication (FPM-DRIFT-001).

findings.title: An absent github.publication decision is classified as 'decision_invalid' rather than 'decision_missing'
evidence: scripts/repo_validation/pr_merge_readiness.py: when decision_path resolves to an existing, parseable, schema-valid human-decisions.yaml that contains no github.publication entry, _matching_github_publication_answer returns False (empty 'matching' list), and the caller appends github_publication_decision_invalid; the None ('missing') branch only fires when decision_path is unresolved or not a file.

tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py test_github_publication_decision_is_required_but_publication_is_optional removes the github.publication decision and asserts github_publication_decision_invalid, so a genuinely missing decision is reported as 'invalid'.

No functional impact: github_publication_decision_invalid and github_publication_decision_missing are both in github_decision_blockers and map to awaiting_human_decision; this is a naming/consistency nit only (FPM-DRIFT-001).

requests_for_additional_verification.request: The human-decisions YAML schema (referenced by _human_decisions_schema(root) and used inside _matching_github_publication_answer via validate_against_schema) is not in changed_files or the packet. _matching_github_publication_answer treats any schema error as False (-> blocking), so please confirm that schema admits the new github.publication decision without modification: decision_id 'github.publication', question_ref 'github.publication', phase_id 'human_merge_decision', classification 'nonblocking-follow-up', and the omission of material_change_id/report_hash that the merge.acceptance decision carries. The verification-gate report claims validate_repo passed, which suggests it is permissive, but this cannot be confirmed read-only from the packet.

The example fixture's updated merge.acceptance report_hash (sha256:6807d1...) and the requirement that human-decisions.yaml top-level run_id equals the report run_id (checked by _matching_github_publication_answer) cannot be recomputed read-only. Confirm the example report's SHA-256 matches the recorded report_hash and that run_id values are aligned, otherwise the 'complete' example would not classify accepted_merge_ready.

_has_matching_human_decision (merge.acceptance) is not included in the diff. Confirm whether it tolerates or rejects multiple merge.acceptance entries; given GEN-001 (dual phases can yield preliminary + final records), verify a stale preliminary acceptance cannot satisfy the gate and ideally that it enforces a single match as _matching_github_publication_answer does for github.publication.

self_declared_limitations: Read-only review: did not run tests, scripts, or the evaluator, and did not access the repository; the claim that targeted tests, full pytest, validate_repo, and diff-check passed was accepted as stated and not independently verified.

Several referenced artifacts are outside the packet and were reasoned about inferentially: the full evaluator bodies for _has_matching_human_decision, _safe_relative, validate_against_schema, and the complete _classify_state ordering; the human-decisions schema; the reviewer-report schema; the unchanged workflow phases; and the example fixtures' run_id/hash context.
Could not recompute SHA-256 hashes or resolve filesystem paths, so hash-binding and evidence-path existence claims in the example/template were assessed structurally, not by execution.

This packet is explicitly a focused source review rather than a full PR-readiness acceptance gate, so acceptance-gate evidence intentionally absent from the packet was not treated as missing mandatory evidence.
